text.title: task-plan-mode-locked-execution Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for task-plan-mode-locked-execution. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: For any change scoped to more than three files OR an estimated runtime over ~5 minutes, the agent MUST enter Plan Mode first. The emitted plan MUST contain four sections in this order: numbered Steps, Verification commands (the exact shell invocations that prove success), Out-of-Scope (explicit exclusions), and Rollback (one-line revert command or "no rollback path" with reason). Approval is recorded as a human comment "approve" or harness-equivalent; the agent MUST NOT exit Plan Mode without it.
rule.rationale: Forcing four sections eliminates the two most common plan failures: missing verification (humans approve plans they cannot test) and missing rollback (humans approve plans they cannot reverse). Read-only enforcement removes the temptation to "start with one quick fix" before approval lands.

rule.statement: After human approval the agent MUST execute the approved plan literally. If the agent discovers the plan is wrong (missing step, infeasible verification, scope underestimate) it MUST stop, post a "PLAN DEVIATION" message naming the discovered issue, and request a new plan. The agent MUST NOT add, remove, reorder, or expand steps without that explicit re-approval. The harness SHOULD assert the diff at end of run is a strict subset of the files named in the approved plan.
rule.rationale: This is the same rule airline pilots follow: deviating from filed flight plan requires ATC clearance, not crew judgment. Without it, an agent that encounters a prompt-injection payload, a missing dependency, or a "while you're there" temptation silently broadens the change — and the next reviewer cannot tell new code from approved code.
